texts.label: text
texts.orig: "The pioneering project shows how innovations and their promotion work together successfully on the way to climate neutrality. I am pleased that another project of the cement industry is supported by the funding programme for the decarbonisation of industry and that we are thus helping this sector to permanently save its process-related emissions in the future.'
texts.text: "The pioneering project shows how innovations and their promotion work together successfully on the way to climate neutrality. I am pleased that another project of the cement industry is supported by the funding programme for the decarbonisation of industry and that we are thus helping this sector to permanently save its process-related emissions in the future.'

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'The knowledge gained on membrane-based CO 2 separation from the funding project can be transferred to other of the 150 Holcim locations worldwide as well as the entire cement industry.'
texts.text: 'The knowledge gained on membrane-based CO 2 separation from the funding project can be transferred to other of the 150 Holcim locations worldwide as well as the entire cement industry.'

texts.label: text
texts.orig: KEI head Dr. Mario Hüttenhofer KEI Press Release July 2023
texts.text: KEI head Dr. Mario Hüttenhofer KEI Press Release July 2023

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After several weeks of running the test facility and completion of the preliminary test phase, the results are extremely positive. "We can observe a good separation rate and comparatively high purities. We are pleased with the very promising results of the first test phase," says Holcim Plant Manager Stephan Hinrichs. "I am very proud that we are one of the first cement plants in the world to gain really practical experience with CO 2 capture.'
texts.text: After several weeks of running the test facility and completion of the preliminary test phase, the results are extremely positive. "We can observe a good separation rate and comparatively high purities. We are pleased with the very promising results of the first test phase," says Holcim Plant Manager Stephan Hinrichs. "I am very proud that we are one of the first cement plants in the world to gain really practical experience with CO 2 capture.'

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'At the Höver cement plant, a promising technology is being tested that can make an important contribution to the decarbonisation of the cement industry in Germany.' BMWK's [German Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action] grant, which is part of the Decarbonisation of Industry funding programme, was made possible by the extraordinarily positive results of the preliminary test phase conducted in 2022.'

table_cells.text: ✗ Energy-intensive regeneration process ✗ Solvents can degrade over time ✗ Reduced efficiency/increased OPEX
✓ No solvents used ✓ Doesn't require energy-intensive regeneration
✗ Solvent emissions ✗ Solvents used can pose safety risks, including the risk of leaks or fires
✓ No solvents used ✓ Much lower safety risk
✗ Large equipment and extensive infrastructure ✗ Challenging when space is limited
✓ Membrane systems have a smaller footprint
✗ Operationally complex ✗ Multiple components, phase changes, and intricate equipment
✓ Simple. Fewer moving parts. Simple to operate ✓ Standard off-the-shelf components
✗ Although proven, not easily scaled up or down, not as flexible for integration into various processes
✓ Modular, more easily scalable and adaptable to different scenarios, from small industrial applications to larger installations
✗ High capital costs, especially when scaling up or retrofitting existing plants
✓ Lower capital costs, particularly for smaller applications or certain industries
